commit 32aead213a56bbbd25b8265b1e07afd3d630c83f
Author: mauricebarnum <maurice.barnum@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 2 03:34:01 2026 -0700

    Implement OTel client instrumentation
    
    Implement the OpenTelemetry instrumentation described in otel-instrumentation-design.md for the Rust Oxia client.
    
    Changes include:
    - Add optional telemetry and go-metrics-compat features, with opentelemetry as an optional metrics API dependency.
    - Add a telemetry module containing operation and batch metrics, Go-compatible metric names, and histogram bucket boundaries matching ext/oxia.
    - Add feature-gated Config meter provider injection so consumers can supply a custom OTel MeterProvider.
    - Instrument Client connect and KV operations with tracing spans and bounded metric attributes.
    - Record operation duration and payload sizes for get, put, range_scan, and other public operations.
    - Record shard-level batch total duration, execution duration, payload size, and request count for read and write batches.
    - Add unit coverage for Go histogram boundary parity and get KeyNotFound success classification.
    
    Validated with:
    - just fmt
    - just lint
    - just build
    - just test
    
    Created with assistance from Codex.
---
 client/Cargo.toml       |   3 +
 client/src/config.rs    |  31 ++-
 client/src/lib.rs       | 636 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-------------
 client/src/shard.rs     | 205 ++++++++++++++--
 client/src/telemetry.rs | 482 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 1155 insertions(+), 202 deletions(-)

index a20cf95..46d739f 100644
--- a/client/Cargo.toml
+++ b/client/Cargo.toml
@@ -27,9 +27,12 @@ dashmap = { version = "6", optional = true }
 fastrand = "2.4.1"
 prost = "0.14.3"
 prost-types = "0.14.3"
+opentelemetry = { version = "0.27.0", features = ["metrics"], optional = true }
 
 [features]
 bench-dashmap = ["dep:dashmap"]
+go-metrics-compat = ["telemetry"]
+telemetry = ["dep:opentelemetry"]
 
 [dev-dependencies]
 tokio = { version = "1.52.3", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros"] }

@@ -99,6 +104,30 @@ impl Config {

         self.retry_on_stale_shard_map

+
+    #[cfg(feature = "telemetry")]
+    #[inline]
+    pub(crate) fn meter_provider(&self) -> Option<&MeterProviderHandle> {
+        self.meter_provider.as_ref()
+    }
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Debug for Config {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        let mut debug = f.debug_struct("Config");
+        debug
+            .field("service_discovery", &"<service discovery>")
+            .field("namespace", &self.namespace)
+            .field("identity", &self.identity)
+            .field("session_timeout", &self.session_timeout)
+            .field("max_parallel_requests", &self.max_parallel_requests)
+            .field("request_timeout", &self.request_timeout)
+            .field("retry", &self.retry)
+            .field("retry_on_stale_shard_map", &self.retry_on_stale_shard_map);
+        #[cfg(feature = "telemetry")]
+        debug.field("meter_provider", &self.meter_provider.is_some());
+        debug.finish()
+    }
 }
 
 impl<S: config_builder::State> ConfigBuilder<S> {

   997	    async fn get_internal(

  1020	            })
  1021	            .await
  1022	        };

[package]
name = "mauricebarnum-oxia-client"
version = "0.1.0"
edition.workspace = true
license = "Apache-2.0"

[dependencies]
mauricebarnum-oxia-common = { path = "../common" }
http = "1"
tokio = { version = "1.52.3", features = ["sync", "rt", "time"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1.18"
tonic = { version = "0.14.6", features = ["gzip", "channel", "zstd"] }
thiserror = "2.0.18"
bytes = "1.11.1"
tracing = "0.1.44"
futures = "0.3.32"
getrandom = "0.4.2"
rand = "0.10.1"
xxhash-rust = { version = "0.8.15", default-features = false, features = [
    "xxh3",
] }
backon = "1.6.0"
arc-swap = "1.9"
tokio-util = "0.7.18"
bon = { version = "3.9.1", features = ["implied-bounds"] }
dashmap = { version = "6", optional = true }
fastrand = "2.4.1"
prost = "0.14.3"
prost-types = "0.14.3"
opentelemetry = { version = "0.27.0", features = ["metrics"], optional = true }

[features]
bench-dashmap = ["dep:dashmap"]
go-metrics-compat = ["telemetry"]
telemetry = ["dep:opentelemetry"]

[dev-dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1.52.3", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros"] }
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3.23", features = ["env-filter", "fmt"] }
chrono = "0.4.44"
socket2 = "0.6.3"
test-log = { version = "0.2.20", features = ["trace", "color"] }
oxia-bin-util = { path = "../oxia-bin-util" }
tempfile = "3.27.0"
anyhow = "1.0.102"
itertools = "0.14.0"
nix = { version = "0.31", default-features = false, features = ["signal"] }

[[bench]]
name = "shard_lookup"
path = "benches/shard_lookup.rs"
harness = false

[[bench]]
name = "channel_pool"
path = "benches/channel_pool.rs"
harness = false

[lints]
workspace = true

[package.metadata.cargo_check_external_types]
allowed_external_types = [

{
  "findings": [
    {
      "title": "[P2] Add the exported OTel type to the external-type allowlist",
      "body": "With the `telemetry` feature enabled, this new public alias and the generated `ConfigBuilder::meter_provider` setter expose `opentelemetry::metrics::MeterProvider`, but `client/Cargo.toml`'s `cargo_check_external_types` allowlist was not updated. In this repo `just lint` runs `cargo check-external-types`, so telemetry-enabled linting will reject the newly exposed external API; add the appropriate OpenTelemetry type(s) to the allowlist or hide the concrete external type from the public API.",
